title: Supine with Legs Elevated or Flexed
content_markdown:
#### Indications

- To improve venous return in patients in “shocked” states.
- To aid in relaxing abdominal muscles in patients with abdominal injuries (legs raised and flexed).

#### Procedure

- Equipment kits and household objects can be utilised to elevate the legs.

- If the patient is on the stretcher, utilise two assistants: 
- 
##### Leg elevation

- Lift the foot end of the stretcher until it locks into leg elevated position.
- To disengage, lift the red tab at the foot of the stretcher and lower the leg section.

- 
##### Legs flexed

- Each operator to grasp the red loop handles on either side of the patient’s knees.
- Lift until the stretcher locks in the legs flexed position.
- To disengaged left the red tab at the foot of the stretcher and lower the leg section.
- Coordinate with the other paramedic to raise legs in one synchronised motion.
- To raise and flex the legs, a pillow or rolled blanket can be used underneath the knees.

Legs elevated

Supine with legs flexed
